The next generation Orb, unveiled at the new world event, offers more advanced technologies that enable World ID proof of human verifications to scale faster and more efficiently than before."},{"url":"https://bitcoinfoundation.org/news/analysis/worldcoin-price-prediction-2026-can-wld-crypto-recover-to-1-and-restart-its-bullish-trend/","title":"Worldcoin Price Prediction 2026: Can WLD Crypto Recover to $1 and Restart Its Bullish Trend? - Bitcoin Foundation","content":"As of June 2026, WLD’s price was down 95% (as measured by CoinGecko) from its all-time high, compared to the price of Bittensor’s TAO▲$215.61, which was down 70% since its all-time high, and the price of Render, which was down 88% since its all-time high.
WLD’s correction, along with FET▲$0.1892, the Artificial Superintelligence Alliance token, falling roughly 95% from its ATH, illustrates how the entire AI token space weakened in the bear market after the 2024 hyper-accumulation cycle.
Later during the year, the German authorities ordered the project to delete some of its iris data, while the Hong Kong government ordered Worldcoin to stop gathering biometric data.
According to Wired, World planned to have deployed 7,500 Orbs in the United States by the end of 2025, with a smaller Orb Mini planned for 2026.
In April 2026, World announced a large update to World ID.
These are important integrations for Worldcoin’s future because they focus on uses of identity beyond token incentives, such as dating apps, video games, enterprise software, e-commerce, and financial products.
Bittensor uses the TAO token to ease the AI network infrastructure and contributions to machine learning, while World ID and World App utilize WLD token for identity verification, onboarding, and expanding the ecosystem.
From a market view, WLD is below its 2024 peak and key resistance levels of $0.80-1.00.
However, whether can Worldcoin recover to $1 in 2026 largely depends on the project’s progress, user adoption, and market sentiment about the future of AI coins in general."},{"url":"https://restofworld.org/2026/sam-altman-worldcoin-zoom-tinder-partnerships/","title":"US tech embraces Sam Altman’s World iris-scan ID banned in places - Rest of World","content":"The company scans people’s irises and assigns them a “proof of humanity.”
October 2021: Co-founders Alex Blania and Sam Altman announce Worldcoin, conceived as a digital identity system linked to a cryptocurrency-based financial network.
They reveal a new device called the Orb, which can scan the irises of around 700 new people per week.
July 2023: Tools for Humanity launches World ID, with plans to install iris-scanning orbs in more than 35 cities across 20 countries.
May 2024: Hong Kong’s data watchdog conducts 10 covert visits at six premises linked to World, and orders the company to cease operations on account of “unnecessary and excessive” data collection.
March 2025: Brazil reaffirms the ban on World, exposing the company to a daily fine of 50,000 reais ($8,800) if it resumes data collection.
April 2026: Worldcoin announces partnerships with Tinder, Zoom, and Docusign."},{"url":"https://world.org/world-id","title":"World ID by World - Digital proof of human for the internet","content":"Today, using World ID is available through integrations with Razer, Match Group, Okta Auth0, and others.
Instead, your account is secured with encrypted backups linked to your login methods (passkey, Google, or Apple)."}]}

A user verifying their identity with a smartphone displaying the World App in front of an Orb. World ID has continued to evolve since this blog was published, learn more about the latest version here. World ID is designed to be the anonymous human layer of the internet built for the age of AI. To help bring World ID access to the millions of people who haven’t yet had a chance to verify at an Orb, World ID 3.0 introduces **World ID Credentials**, a new way to enable the World Network to scale and add utility even faster**.**. Starting today in the US, Malaysia and the UK and expanding in the coming weeks to many more countries around the world, World ID holders can now optionally store information from their **physical NFC-enabled passports** in World App on their device—with none of the data stored off their device or accessible to World Foundation, TFH or any third party—then use their World ID to prove things like age, nationality and unique passport ownership without revealing their real identity.","score":0.54564947,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://siliconangle.com/2024/10/17/sam-altmans-worldcoin-rebrands-world-launches-updated-iris-scanning-orb","title":"Sam Altman's Worldcoin rebrands as World and launches ...","content":"Oct 17, 2024 — Other announcements made today include the launch of World ID 3.0, which introduces new features like World ID Credentials and Deep Face.","score":0.5376489,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://cryptoslate.com/worldcoin-rebrands-as-world-targets-1-billion-users-with-new-blockchain-and-biometric-tech","title":"Worldcoin rebrands as World, targets 1 billion users with new blockchain and biometric tech","content":"The updates included the launch of World Chain, an Ethereum-secured Layer 2 blockchain, and new versions of its biometric Orb devices and World App. Worldcoin rebrands as World, targets 1 billion users with new blockchain and biometric tech. Worldcoin rebrands as World, targets 1 billion users with new blockchain and biometric tech. Worldcoin, the Sam Altman-backed digital identity initiative, announced a major rebrand as World and unveiled ambitious plans to grow its user base to 1 billion people during a keynote address on Oct. 17. The keynote, led by co-founders Altman and Alex Blania, marked a significant shift in the project's trajectory after it announced a series of key updates designed to drive global adoption of its decentralized identity platform. The updates included the launch of World Chain, an Ethereum-secured Layer 2 blockchain, and the unveiling of a new version of the project's biometric Orb devices. World's new version of the Orb, which scans a user's iris to verify their identity, is central to the project's ambition to grow its global footprint.","score":0.50329643,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://idtechwire.com/world-digital-id-platform-launches-in-six-us-cities-plans-major-expansion","title":"World Digital ID Platform Launches in Six US Cities, Plans Major Expansion - ID Tech","content":"# World Digital ID Platform Launches in Six US Cities, Plans Major Expansion. World, the digital identity verification platform formerly known as Worldcoin, has officially launched operations in the United States.
